2606.03883.title: Reasoning Structure of Large Language Models
2606.03883.paper_summary: Large reasoning models (LRMs) are often evaluated using metrics such as final-answer accuracy or token count. However, identical scores on these metrics can hide fundamentally different reasoning structures. To address this limitation, we introduce a scalable LRM benchmark of logic puzzles and a pipeline that converts unstructured traces into verifiable reasoning graphs of claims and dependencies. This turns reasoning into a structured, measurable object whose topology can be quantitatively analyzed. Building on this, we define a reasoning efficiency metric that quantifies how concentrated the model's logical flow is. Our analysis on open-source reasoning models shows that structural measurements separate behaviors that token count and accuracy conflate, providing a practical tool for diagnosing failure modes and comparing how reasoning scales with puzzle difficulty.
2606.03883.paper_summary_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）通常使用最終答案準確性或標記數等指標進行評估。<lb/>然而，這些指標上的相同分數可能掩蓋了根本不同的推理結構。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們引入了一個可擴展的邏輯謎題LRM基準和一個將非結構化痕跡轉換為可驗證的主張和依賴關係推理圖的流程。<lb/>這將推理轉變為一個結構化、可測量的對象，其拓撲結構可以進行定量分析。<lb/>在此基礎上，我們定義了一個推理效率指標，用於量化模型邏輯流的集中程度。<lb/>我們對開源推理模型的分析顯示，結構測量可以區分標記數和準確性混淆的行為，提供了一個實用工具來診斷失效模式並比較推理如何隨著謎題難度的增加而擴展。
2606.03883.author: Frédéric Berdoz et.al.
2606.03883.authors: Frédéric Berdoz, Luca A. Lanzendörfer, Fabian Farestam, Roger Wattenhofer
